source_alignment.subject: Science
source_alignment.topic: Properties, mixtures and changes of materials
source_alignment.source_statement: Compare and group everyday materials on the basis of their properties; know that some materials dissolve in liquid to form a solution and describe how to recover a substance from a solution; use knowledge of solids, liquids and gases to decide how mixtures might be separated; give reasons for uses of everyday materials; and demonstrate that dissolving, mixing and changes of state are reversible changes while explaining that some changes result in new materials and are not usually reversible.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack integrates property testing, fair comparison, material selection, solution and mixture separation, and reversible versus irreversible change. It separates observation from inference, requires evidence-bounded claims and uses virtual or teacher-supervised investigations with explicit safety and disposal limits.

objective.child_goal: I can test materials, use evidence to choose them, separate mixtures and explain whether changes can be reversed.
objective.statement: Compare material properties, justify uses, investigate dissolving and separation, and classify changes using observations and particle-informed models.
misconceptions: Assumes a material has one fixed usefulness instead of matching several relevant properties to a purpose.
Thinks a dissolved solid has disappeared, melted or become part of the liquid permanently.
Chooses a separation method by naming equipment rather than considering particle size, solubility, magnetism or state.
Treats every change that looks dramatic as irreversible, or every physical rearrangement as easy to reverse completely.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A feature of a material that can be observed or tested, such as hardness, transparency or conductivity.
vocabulary.audio_script: A property is evidence about how a material behaves under stated conditions.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Material cards connected to labelled test results.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A mixture formed when a soluble substance dissolves evenly in a liquid.
vocabulary.audio_script: In a solution, the solute is still present even when its particles are too small to see.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A before-and-after model showing dispersed solute particles.

vocabulary.child_explanation: To recover parts of a mixture using differences in their properties.
vocabulary.audio_script: A separation method works because the substances have a useful difference in property.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Mixture components taking different routes through a method diagram.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A change where the original material or materials can be recovered.
vocabulary.audio_script: A reversible change can be undone, although the recovery may require a process and may not be perfect in a classroom test.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A two-way arrow between starting and changed states.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A change that forms new material or materials and is not usually changed back easily.
vocabulary.audio_script: Evidence of new materials can support an irreversible-change explanation.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A one-way arrow with new-property evidence.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Sort each statement into observation, measurement or inference.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Separate direct evidence from explanations before material claims are made.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Say what was seen or measured first. Then give the explanation that the evidence supports.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three labelled columns receive short property and change statements.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Use tested properties to choose materials, separation routes and change explanations.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce properties as the connecting idea across uses, mixtures and changes.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The useful question is not simply what a material is called, but how it behaves in the conditions that matter.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A material evidence hub branches to CHOOSE, SEPARATE and EXPLAIN.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Plan how to separate salt and sand by using their different solubility and particle behaviour.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model a multi-stage separation route and explain why each method works.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Dissolve the salt in water, filter out the insoluble sand, then recover salt from the solution by evaporation in a controlled model.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A flow diagram tracks salt, sand and water through dissolve, filter and evaporate stages.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose a question, control variables, collect repeated results and justify a material or process from the evidence.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Coordinate property testing, fair comparison, data recording and cautious conclusion.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Test the property that matters, keep the comparison fair and limit the conclusion to the materials and conditions tested.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A PREDICT-TEST-RECORD-EXPLAIN lab links controls, a results table and a claim-evidence panel.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Sugar is no longer visible after stirring in water. What evidence could show that it is still present?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Challenge disappearance and melting explanations for dissolving.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The solution's mass and the recovery of sugar after water removal can show that the dissolved substance remains.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A balance record and particle model stay constant while visible grains disperse.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach your companion why one separation method works and how evidence distinguishes a reversible from an irreversible change.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Gather causal explanation and evidence-quality signals.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Name the property difference, the evidence and one limit on the claim.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A separation route and two-way or one-way change model remain visible during explanation.

manipulatives.purpose: Compare hardness, transparency, flexibility, thermal conductivity, electrical conductivity, magnetism and response to water under controlled conditions.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners choose a relevant test, lock control variables, collect repeat measurements and use a weighted property profile to recommend a material for a stated purpose.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: All tests have virtual data-table and teacher-demonstration modes; controls use buttons and numeric inputs; visual effects have spoken descriptions; no learner handles electricity, heat, glass or unknown substances; and colour is never the sole result code.

manipulatives.purpose: Design and explain single- and multi-stage separation routes using sieving, filtering, magnets, decanting and evaporation models.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners inspect component properties, order method cards, predict where each substance goes and revise the route when a method cannot separate the chosen components.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Every route is duplicated as a numbered text list; cards have move menus; particle size, solubility, state and magnetism are announced; animations can be replaced by before/after diagrams; and unsafe practical actions are simulation-only.

manipulatives.purpose: Compare reversible physical changes with changes that form new materials using observation, recovery evidence and model limitations.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners sort evidence such as state change, gas production, temperature change, colour change and recoverability, then write a cautious conclusion.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Evidence is available as text, icon and audio; sensory descriptions avoid requiring smell or taste; surprising changes do not flash; oral responses are supported; and uncertainty or insufficient-evidence choices are always available.

practice.interleaving_notes: Secure observable property vocabulary and single-method separations first; then mix fair tests, multi-stage routes, solutions, material selection and reversible/irreversible evidence while preserving explicit safety and model-boundary notes.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Compare materials fairly and justify a choice using multiple properties relevant to a purpose.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: design brief + 3-5 candidate materials + controlled test data + required/undesired property weights + recommendation and limitation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use plausible property data and avoid absolute claims such as strongest or best without a defined test and comparison set.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Explain dissolving as formation of a solution and use evidence to show the solute remains and may be recovered.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: solute-solvent scenario + mass/visibility/temperature observations + particle model + recovery method + evidence-bounded explanation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep dissolving distinct from melting; do not imply all dissolved substances are safely recoverable or that evaporation should be attempted unsupervised.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Select and sequence separation methods from differences in particle size, solubility, magnetism and state.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: 2-4 component mixture + property table + method cards + destination tracking + route explanation + recovery check
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Verify that every proposed route can work under the stated conditions; filtration must not be shown separating a dissolved solute.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Classify changes from recovery and new-material evidence rather than appearance alone.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: before/during/after observations + recovery attempt/model + evidence cards + reversible/irreversible/insufficient conclusion
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid treating one sign such as colour or temperature change as proof by itself; distinguish practical difficulty from scientific irreversibility at age-appropriate depth.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain property, solution, separation and change reasoning through short claim-evidence critiques.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue + concise claim + observation or data snippet + support/challenge/insufficient choice + corrected explanation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep reading demand below the science demand and include uncertainty when evidence does not justify a causal or universal claim.

body.prompt: A viewing panel must let light through, resist water and keep its shape. Which evidence best supports choosing clear acrylic over absorbent card?
choices: Acrylic matches all three required properties in the stated tests
Acrylic is always the best material for every purpose
Card is natural, so its tested properties do not matter
Both materials are identical because both can be flat

expected_answer.value: Acrylic matches all three required properties in the stated tests

hints: Match each requirement to a test result.
Avoid a claim that goes beyond this purpose and these materials.
question_variants.explanation: For this viewing panel, the acrylic is supported by evidence that it is transparent, waterproof and rigid; this does not make it best for every use.

body.prompt: Salt is stirred into water until no crystals are visible. Which statement is best supported?
observations: total mass is unchanged in a closed container
salt can be recovered after water is removed in a controlled model
choices: The salt dissolved and remains in the solution
The salt vanished and no longer exists
The salt melted because all liquids are hot

expected_answer.value: The salt dissolved and remains in the solution

hints: Use the mass and recovery evidence.
Not being visible does not mean the substance is absent.
question_variants.explanation: The unchanged mass and later recovery support the explanation that salt particles are dispersed through the solution rather than gone.

body.prompt: A mixture contains iron filings, sand and salt. Choose a valid route to recover the three solids.
choices: Use a magnet; add water; filter; evaporate the filtrate in a controlled model
Filter the dry mixture once and all three solids separate
Evaporate the dry mixture before adding water
Add water and filter; the dissolved salt stays with the sand on the filter

expected_answer.value: Use a magnet; add water; filter; evaporate the filtrate in a controlled model

hints: After salt dissolves, which solid is trapped by a filter?
question_variants.explanation: Magnetism separates iron; water dissolves salt but not sand; filtration retains sand; removing water from the filtrate recovers salt.

body.prompt: Wax melts and later solidifies. A different sample burns and produces smoke and ash. Which conclusion best fits?
evidence: burning produces materials with different properties
ash cannot be changed back into the original wax by cooling
choices: Melting is reversible; burning is not usually reversible and forms new materials
Both are irreversible because both look different
Both are reversible because both started with wax
Melting forms ash and burning forms liquid wax

expected_answer.value: Melting is reversible; burning is not usually reversible and forms new materials

hints: Ask whether the original material can be recovered.
Look for evidence that new materials formed.
question_variants.explanation: Cooling can recover solid wax after melting, while burning produces smoke and ash with new properties and is not usually reversible.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Assumes a material has one fixed usefulness instead of matching several relevant properties to a purpose.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner recommends a material from one familiar use, appearance or unqualified label such as strong without using test conditions.
repair_sequence: List the purpose's required and unwanted properties.
Compare only relevant test evidence across candidates.
State the recommendation and one limit on the claim.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner justifies a new material choice with at least two relevant properties and bounded language.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks a dissolved solid has disappeared, melted or become part of the liquid permanently.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner says invisible solute no longer exists or selects melting despite no state-change evidence.
repair_sequence: Compare total mass before and after in a closed model.
Show solute particles dispersed between solvent particles.
Use a recovery model and distinguish dissolving from melting.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner explains a new solution using conservation, dispersal and possible recovery evidence.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Chooses a separation method by naming equipment rather than considering particle size, solubility, magnetism or state.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The chosen method cannot distinguish the mixture components, such as filtering a dissolved solute from water.
repair_sequence: Build a property table for every component.
Choose the first property difference that one method can exploit.
Track each component and repeat until the recovery goal is met.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner designs and explains a valid route for a fresh two- or three-component mixture.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats every change that looks dramatic as irreversible, or every physical rearrangement as easy to reverse completely.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner classifies from appearance alone or ignores recovery and new-material evidence.
repair_sequence: Ask whether original materials can be recovered and whether new properties indicate new materials.
Choose reversible, irreversible or insufficient evidence and state practical limits.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner classifies a new pair of changes using recovery and new-material evidence rather than visual drama.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a calm virtual laboratory with no strong sound effects, no simulated fumes, one active test at a time and predictable transitions.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace dissolving, filtering, evaporation and change animations with numbered still frames, particle diagrams and complete result tables.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Announce material, test condition, observation, measurement and conclusion separately, with replay at every evidence point.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use short method cards, icons plus words for properties, glossary popovers, sentence frames and a linear alternative to flow diagrams.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Display QUESTION-PREDICT-TEST-RECORD-EXPLAIN or MIXTURE-PROPERTY-METHOD-CHECK strips and lock irrelevant controls.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Remove timers, preserve data, permit repeated virtual trials, expose all graph values in tables and allow oral, typed or structured responses.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Treat predictions as testable, credit accurate observations before explanations and preserve correct route stages during repair.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach transparent, flexible, conductor, insulator, soluble, solution, filter, evaporate, reversible and irreversible with objects, diagrams and sentence frames.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires an evidence-based material choice, a valid separation method and a reversible/irreversible explanation in at least two formats. Secure requires fair-test control, a multi-stage separation or solution-recovery explanation, and a cautious claim that distinguishes observation from inference on two retained sessions.
evidence.parent_summary: Can test and compare materials, choose them for a purpose, explain solutions and separation, and use evidence to decide whether changes are reversible.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Stores test design, observations, measurements, method-property links, recovery evidence and conclusions separately so scientific reasoning is distinguishable from reading, memory or motor demands.
next_actions: Route unsupported material choices to required-property profiles and bounded claims.
Route disappearance explanations to mass, particle and recovery evidence.
Route invalid separation routes to component property tracking.
Schedule mixed properties, solutions, separation and change retrieval at 1, 3, 7, 14 and 30 days.

qa.notes: Deep review-stage Year 5 pack. Human review should verify scientific accuracy, material data, separation feasibility, particle-model limits, reversible/irreversible wording, safe virtual or teacher-supervised procedures, disposal language and complete keyboard, screen-reader and static investigation routes before promotion.
